_meta.schema: III ADR-003 §2 local learning store — ACCUMULATE writes target this file; canonical upstream (III.aDNA, md5 5adb0dfa38d9224649c3b2cba83852ae) is read-only from the consumer side
_meta.graduation: frequency>=3 across >=2 sessions + acceptance>=0.80 + Stanley/Argus gate (III ADR-003 §3)

summary: Producer input carries a human label per source (e.g. brief_consumer / document_generator sources[].label) but the source degrades to a bare URL `link` node in the .canvas (baseline JSON Canvas link nodes have no anchor-text slot), so provenance is present in-spec yet renders as a bare URL with no visible label.
proposed_fix: Producer-side: emit an adjacent caption/text node carrying the label, or fold the label into _reserved.component_types[node].qualities.label; OR a Standard-side errata defining a link-label carry in _reserved. Do not force a label into the baseline link node.
resolved_by: Errata sweep (session_stanley_20260620_221404_post_keystone_tail): folded the authored sources[].label into _reserved.component_types[node].qualities.label in document_generator/blocks.py + brief_consumer/consume.py (guarded on a non-empty label; not forced into the baseline link node). Examples + the document_small golden regenerated (surgical: only the qualities.label added); suites green 10/16/37, canvas_std firewall git-diff 0.
residual: Visible-label rendering still depends on the PT-P5 render loop honoring qualities.label — the baseline link node stays anchor-text-less by design. A Standard-side _reserved link-label carry remains an option, not required now.
note: Local candidate; mitigated producer-side in the post-Keystone errata sweep. The 3rd session is the resolution, not a recurrence (frequency held at 2); not graduated to III canonical (frequency<3 + needs acceptance>=0.80 + Stanley/Argus gate).

summary: The document_generator (E4.1) lays content out as a single-pass deterministic vertical stack with fixed page boxes; it does not reflow or re-paginate, so a content-heavy page can overflow its page{p} bounds. Conformance is unaffected (geometry is positional and pages stay within doc_root), but visual fidelity needs real pagination.
proposed_fix: Add reflow/auto-pagination (measure content, break pages) as an E4.2 format-contract concern (length_window -> page breaks) and a PT-P5 render concern; E4.1 ships the fixed-stack skeleton deliberately.
resolved_by: E4.2 section-level reflow (layout.paginate): a content-heavy model page is paginated across canvas pages, each <= CONTENT_H; whitepaper 2->5, grant 1->4. Verified by test_reflow.py.
residual: intra_section_pagination — a single section taller than a whole page still overflows its own page (flagged qualities.layout_note='oversized_overflow'); widow/orphan + mid-section breaks are PT-P5 render concerns.
note: Producer-layout limitation surfaced by the first long-form consumer (E4.1); ADDRESSED by E4.2 reflow at section granularity; narrow residual tracked under PT P5. Not a Standard gap.
